description: Upstream BMS (/SESOWABMSPD04) polled the wrong register for ~87 days. Raw hourly samples alternate between the real counter (~7k MWh band), a near-zero artifact (<100), and a stuck-high value (~805k). At daily V_LAST aggregation only ~5 of 87 corruption-window days landed in the real-register band. The stitched :d is produced by a 3-segment split with an interpolate patch across the corruption window: :d.A (slice valid_to=2025-07-15) + :d.patch (interpolate valid_from=2025-07-15 valid_to=2025-10-09, source=:d.raw) + :d.B (slice valid_from=2025-10-09). interpolate uses the last clean reading BEFORE valid_from (2025-07-14=7187.17) and the first reading AT/AFTER valid_to (2025-10-09=7671.14) as endpoints and linearly fills the 86-day gap at ~5.56 MWh/day — cumulative preserved exactly at both boundaries, no artifact values participate. Counter is monotone over the whole year; annual integral is best-effort linear. Prior approaches tried and rejected: (a) bracket on :d.raw kept only samples in the endpoint value-range but rolling_sum's concat-with-anchor-offset stitch sort of all source rows can't compose a bracket-overlay with a full-period raw source — produces artefacts at alternating in-band/out-of-band samples; (b) hourly ingestion (53 of 87 corruption-window days have ≥1 real-register sample in the hourly extract at reference/snowflake_meter_readings/B217.Å1_VM71.csv) would allow bracket to keep real samples but requires ingesting hourly data and changing the ontology's aggregate field. interpolate was chosen because it matches existing primitives and doesn't depend on how many samples survive inside the window; only the two outside-window endpoints matter.
Counter froze 2026-02-19 at value 12121.072. Ontology stitches raw segment (:d.raw, valid_to 2026-02-19) with a children-sum patch (:d.patch, sum of B217.Å1_VMM71:d.D) via rolling_sum.

Children-sum patch: recovers B216 consumption from its sole child B217.Å1_VMM71 after B216's counter froze. Assumes B217 captures all B216 downstream flow; the ~28 MWh Feb-2026 vs-Excel delta is the pre-freeze (Feb 1 → Feb 18) portion that Excel's STRUX register-diff absorbed as zero.
Counter frozen at 12737.1533 for the entire observed window; last real reading predates the 2025-01-01 ingest start. Patched from child B327.Å1_VMM71 via sum from 2026-02-07 onward.

Children-sum patch: B327.Å1_VMM71 (sole child) stands in for B308's frozen counter. Produces meter_net ≈ 0 (as children equal parent). Feb-2026 Excel shows −30.78 vs ontology −8.98 MWh; the ~22 MWh gap is pre-patch Feb-1 → Feb-7 that the ontology does not reconstruct.

Trunk ÅNGA counter frozen at 2227.06 from 2025-01-01 through late October 2025 (+0.57 MWh drift over 10 months; effectively dead register), then unfreezes and ticks normally from 2025-11-01 onward. B307.Å1_VMM71 is a pool meter whose hasSubMeter children (B216, B302, B304, B311, B334, B337, B339) report real consumption throughout the freeze window, so meter_net on B307.Å1_VMM71 reads strongly negative (≈ −1500 to −2600 MWh/month, Σ ≈ −22 GWh) across Jan–Oct 2025. Not patched: true pre-November trunk consumption is unknown; patching to zero would mask the magnitude of the gap, and interpolating 2227 → the 2025-11-27 value of 8834 would spread ~6.6 GWh backward across a window where Excel itself reads zero. The former ann-snv-anga-b307-vmm71-swap annotation mis-categorised the 2025-07-31 segment boundary as a device swap: the register was already frozen pre-swap and continues flat post-swap until November, so the split straddles a non-event. Split kept in place; rolling_sum stitching is harmless on continuous values.

Post-unfreeze ontology totals lag Excel by ~3 months. Ontology ticks Nov=7.5, Dec=9.7, Jan=2.0, Feb=9.3 GWh. Excel shows zero until Feb 2026, then Feb=7.8, Mar=10.9, Apr=0.26 GWh — same cumulative ~27 GWh but shifted ~3 months later. Source of the offset unknown: delayed Excel invoicing, wrong Snowflake timestamps, or a genuine mid-window meter commissioning that accounting dated differently from BMS. Needs site verification before any corrective action.
Device register swap on 2025-07-31. Handled by 2-segment split :d.A | :d.B stitched via rolling_sum.
Device swap on 2026-01-13 (last value 3019.23). Handled by 2-segment split :d.A | :d.B stitched via rolling_sum.

Counter went offline 2025-07-31 at 328545. Downstream of B325 Panna2; no children to patch from — consumption beyond this date is unrecoverable from B390 alone. Leaf meter relative to its intended topology branch.
Counter went offline 2025-07-25 at 133.0. Leaf meter; no children available for a sum-patch.

Excel row 23 (B307 pool formula) subtracts B330.Å1_VMM71, and row 46 (B337) subtracts the same meter — B330 is double-counted in the Excel totals. Ontology attaches B330 to B337 per decisions.md 2026-04-20; B307 therefore over-counts in topology by Δ(B330) each month (~0.9 MWh Jan-2026, ~27 MWh Feb-2026). Excel is immutable; this discrepancy is expected and permanent.

Direction conflict between flow_schema and naming extractor for the B311.Å1_VMM70 ↔ B311.Å1_VMM71 edge. Not yet resolved in decisions.md. Does not affect Jan/Feb 2026 totals because both meters are leaf-level relative to their building's accounting.

Bracket patch: parameter-free monotone value-range clip over :d.raw's readings. Endpoints (v_lo, v_hi) derive from the source's readings immediately outside the validity window — the last clean pre-corruption value and the first clean post-corruption value. Any reading whose value falls outside [v_lo, v_hi] is physically impossible for a monotone counter and is dropped.

Counter offline 2025-09-21 through 2026-01-13 (~116 days), full catchup flush on 2026-01-14 (+135.62 kWh). Total preserved: 135.75 kWh ÷ 116 days = 1.17 kWh/day, matching neighbor rates (~1.2/day). Patched via interpolate between clean endpoints 2025-09-20 (483.323) and 2026-01-14 (619.070).

Partial data loss: counter flat 2025-07-14 through 2025-07-23 (~10 days), resumed with only +2.7 kWh caught up across Jul 24-25. Pre/post neighbor rate ~1.85/day suggests ~22 kWh expected; ~19 kWh was lost (counter stopped counting, not just reporting). Not patched via interpolate — that would under-distribute. Magnitude is small (~0.05% of annual) so accepted as-is.
Counter rollover at Schneider ceiling (~10,000,000). Counter reset from 9999063 on 2025-12-03 (Δ=-9996275). Readings resume normally. Stitched via rolling_sum of :d.A + :d.B segments.
Counter rollover at Schneider ceiling (~10,000,000). Counter reset from 9998599 on 2025-09-15 (Δ=-9994911). Stitched via rolling_sum.
Counter rollover at Schneider ceiling (~10,000,000). Counter reset from 9998597 on 2025-06-08. Stitched via rolling_sum.

Counter rollover at Schneider ceiling (~10,000,000). Counter reset from 9996659 on 2025-05-21. Stitched via rolling_sum. Swap well before comparison window.

Brings B334 from -100% to +7% — small residual (+30000 kWh/mo) is genuine STRUX-vs-BMS fidelity gap, not a topology bug.

Complex formula row 48 (B339): trunk summaries T71-1..T78-1 + 4 sub-feeders. Added hasSubMeter edges trunk→sub (4 edges). Building matches within 0-106 kWh.

B317 Excel Feb cached value (134118 kWh) is 2× Jan (62941 kWh) despite similar T49 trunk flow in both months. Ontology gives 58643 Feb, consistent with T49 Feb flow and matching Jan's scale. Likely Excel Feb data-entry anomaly; flagged for human review.

B334.T87, T88, T89, T91, T92 lack Snowflake readings. Applied a derived timeseries with aggregation=sum sourcing from their Snowflake sub-feeders (underscore notation: T87_1_1, T87_2_2, etc.). Bridged B334 from -100% to +7.2% — remaining delta is the natural STRUX-vs-BMS measurement fidelity gap. 20 BMS sub-meter IDs added to facit_meters at campus attribution.

SNV EL monthly reconciliation rows marked `match` may show non-zero diffs up to ~10 kWh (relative < 0.005%). These are floating-point accumulation artifacts: an ontology building total is computed by summing daily V_LAST deltas across multiple meters through LAG subtraction in measured_flow, while Excel summarizes from STRUX-cached monthly values that come from the same source with slightly different rounding semantics. Per-building noise scales with meter count. Not a DQ issue; `match` is the honest classification. Rows with diff ≥ 10 kWh are individually retagged `ontology_drift` with the specific cause (typically rolling_sum boundary-day loss at a documented rollover/swap, or a derived_sum patch against STRUX-cached Excel values).

Excel double-subtracts this meter: it appears as a subtracted −term on row 26 (B310 formula) AND on row 30 (B314 formula). Ontology enforces one-parent-per-meter and attached it to B314 (shorter-formula-wins). B310 therefore over-counts vs Excel by Δ(B313.KV1_VMM22_V) each month (+120.82 MWh Jan-2026, +26.73 MWh Feb-2026). Excel is immutable.

Excel lists B315.KV1_VMM21_V as both a +term on row 30 (B314) AND the sole +term on row 31 (B315). Ontology attached the meter to B315 (prefix-match convention). Result: B311 over- counts by +23.72 Jan / +15.22 Feb (via the pool share), and B314 under-counts by the same magnitude. B315 itself matches Excel exactly. Excel is immutable.

Four meter refs carry tenant/pool splits from the SNV kyla Excel sheet. Rows 19 (B302) and 20 (B303) contain `0.5 × B304.KB2` on the S column — tenant split of a single physical meter between two adjacent buildings. Rows 22 (B305) and 23 (B307) contain `B305.KB1 + 0.5 × B307.KB1` and `B307.KB1_VMM52 + 0.5 × B307.KB1` respectively — the coefficient lives on the second + term (T column) in those rows, while the S term is a building's own full-attribution meter. Ontology encoding: emit `feeds` edges with flow_coefficient on the edge (B304.KB2 → B302/B303 @ 0.5/0.5; B307.KB1 → B305/B307 @ 0.5/0.5; B305.KB1 → B305 @ 1.0; B307.KB1_VMM52 → B307 @ 1.0). Because Σk = 1.0 for each source, the source meters are attributed to campus (`building_id=''`) per the fully-split convention — numerically identical to attributing the physical meter to its first + building (since `meter_net = flow × (1 − Σk) = 0` when fully split). Residuals of ±1–3 MWh expected on B302/B303/B305/B307 because `views.sql::meter_net` has no exact fractional-subtract primitive for the 0.5 case. Classification: `excel_cooked_coefficient`.

The Excel sjövatten sheet uses a virtual residual meter "BPS_V2" (= B342.V2_VM90_V + B342.V2_VM91_V − 15 direct consumer meters) that is fractionally distributed to FIVE buildings with R-factors summing to 1.0: B301 @ 0.09, B302 @ 0.18, B303 @ 0.18, B307 @ 0.46, B344 @ 0.09. Coefficients vary month-to-month in the live sheet's hidden R-column; a static-coefficient approximation would be acceptable if the drain side were complete. 2026-04-21 investigation: attempted to materialise a BPS_V2 virtual-meter pool (1 SNV-campus virtual + 5 building share virtuals, 15 hasSubMeter drains, 2 inlet refs summed via a derived sum-ref, 5 outgoing feeds at the R-factors). Mechanics worked (sum over sum-ref inlet produces single-row-per-timestamp flow; multi-feed double-count bug avoided by aggregating inlets at the timeseries layer). BUT: 8 of the 15 direct-consumer meters have no Snowflake data (STRUX-only: B304-52-V2-AW026, B310.V2_GF4_1, B330.V2_GF4_1, B337.V2_GF4_1, TE-52-V2-GF4:1 Kringlan, TE-52-V2-SCANIA; offline pre-2026-01: B305.V2_VMM21, B339.V2_GF4), so the ontology-computed residual is ~34× larger than Excel's (~204k vs 6k m³ in Jan 2026). Building shares came out ~34× too large, which is worse than the pre-existing zero-attribution. Reverted. Re-attempt becomes viable once the 8 STRUX-only/offline meters have Snowflake counter data. Jan-2026 residuals (ontology vs Excel): B301 −540, B302 −1081, B303 −1081, B307 −2761, B344 −540 kWh. Classify as excel_cooked_coefficient.

B304-52-V2-AW026 is a manually-read STRUX meter with no Snowflake equivalent. Excel caches the meter's monthly value; ontology reads zero. B304 therefore under-counts by the STRUX cached value each month (−29640 Jan-2026, −43483 Feb-2026). Per project convention, STRUX-only meters are never synthesized as Snowflake rows.

Counter went offline 2025-08-05 at value 335549.97. Replaced with a new device that came online 2025-11-30 at pre-loaded baseline value 42668.71 (factory / commission offset, not consumption). Handled by 2-segment split :d.A | :d.B stitched via rolling_sum; the classifier treats the boundary as a device swap (anchor to new raw, cumulative held flat across the boundary) rather than a reset, so the 42,668 baseline is not mistakenly recorded as one-day consumption. The ~4-month gap (Aug 5 → Nov 29) is flat-carried; physical flow during that period is unrecovered. Outside Jan/Feb 2026 reference period so no facit impact.

Seawater cooling seasonal pattern. Active Jun-Oct (~40-90 L/day summer peak), minimal Nov-May (mostly flat days with occasional 1-2 L/day). Not a data-quality issue — reflects physical use of seawater cooling during warm months.

Inverse seasonal pattern. Active Dec-May (~50-115k L/month), zero Jun-Nov (6-month dormancy). Likely a winter-use circuit (possibly heat-recovery or free-cooling dependent on seawater temperature). Not a data-quality issue. The 'spike' visible on 2026-02-28 is simply the normal daily rate during its active season.
Device swap 2025-08-05 (last value 67.26). Handled by 2-segment split B201.VP1_VMM61:d.A | :d.B via rolling_sum.

Register-read corruption / pre-deployment artifacts pre-2025-10-09. :d.A has 13 days of zero readings then a stuck-high artifact at 662831.21 for 8 days (same pattern as the B217.Å1_VMM71 ÅNGA register corruption — wrong register polled). :d.B is a further 5-day artifact at 23724. Neither segment is trustworthy. Previously handled as a multi-swap rolling_sum producing a compounded ~687 GWh stitched total, which was nonsense given the meter's real ~4 kWh/day rate. Canonical :d now slices from :d.C alone (valid_from=2025-10-09), accepting that pre-Oct-9 data is unrecoverable. :d.A and :d.B retained as raw refs for audit. 2025 annual is missing pre-Oct-9 consumption; Jan/Feb 2026 facit comparisons unaffected.

B310 VARME formula (row 22): +VP2_VMM61 +VP1_VMM61 +VS2_VMM61 +VS2_VMM62 − 22 sub-meters across B301-B385. Sum of children exceeds parent side by ~2.5 MWh/yr (~200 kWh/month), producing steady negative residual all months. Two identified causes: (1) Excel bug — B385.VP2_VMM62 subtracted here (col Z) but never +added to B385's formula (row 64 only adds VP2_VMM61), losing ~106 kWh/yr from the accounting; (2) remaining ~2.4 MWh/yr gap likely calibration drift or unmetered physical bypass feeding sub-buildings without going through B310's four parent meters. Ontology mirrors Excel exactly. Needs facilities confirmation to determine if parent metering coverage is physically complete.
